<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p>I don't care if you're "the next Supreme"...</p><p>I don't care if you're about to take "the hairdressing community by storm "...</p><p>Or if you're a caffein induced stylist that can "pull from Telfar"... </p><p>Or if you're a COVID-inspired fashion photographer...</p><p>Or if you're the "Trap Bob Dylan"...</p><p>Or if you're the only architect who's mastered the timeless principles of Christopher Alexander...</p><p>You are nothing.</p><p>Without your fans.</p><p>You will not advance.</p><p>Without your fans.</p><p>Put this up on your wall where you can see it every day:</p><h2>YOUR CAREER IS NOT YOUR ART. YOUR CAREER IS YOUR RELATIONSHIP TO YOUR FANS.</h2><p>I know, I know.</p><p>You think that what you do is "special".</p><p>But it's only gonna be "special" when there's a passionate group of people who says it is.</p><p>And I don't mean your family or your friends. </p><p>I mean the people who become your NEW "family and friends" BECAUSE OF your art.</p><p>I don't just mean the people who buy what you do.</p><p>I also mean the people who will be on your team one day to help you promote it.</p><p>For example:</p><p>I meet so many photographers trying to get known.</p><p>They put their pics on Instagram.</p><p>It's their "portfolio", they say.</p><p>Dear photographers, Did you know that 95 million photos get uploaded every day on Instagram?</p><p>Why should we care about yours?</p><p>Ok, I'll come back to that in a second.</p><p>Another statistic to make you weep: </p><p>4000 songs get added to Spotify every day. </p><p>I meet singers and producers trying to get known. Some even have labels behind them.</p><p>Um, tell me: What about YOUR new single makes it something that I have to hear before I die?</p><p>You see, THAT'S the problem.</p><p>You can't answer that question in one sentence.</p><p>You can't answer that question with conviction.</p><p>You can't give me an answer that makes me start typing your name into Spotify while you're still talking.</p><p>You have no story.</p><p>You have no message.</p><p>If you had either of those things, you'd be creating better odds for yourself. You'd have your own lane. </p><p>Let me show you how--</p><p>No seriously. Let's do the math.</p><p>We'll use music as an example.</p><p><strong>But this applies to anyone out there with a brand.</strong></p><p>So... 4,000 songs released a day, right? Of those 4,000 releases, how many of them are "pop artists"?</p><p>Maybe 2000.</p><p>How many of them are "pop artists who rap"?</p><p>Maybe 500.</p><p>How many are "pop artists who rap exclusively about female empowerment"?</p><p>Maybe 150.</p><p>How many are "pop artists who rap exclusively about female empowerment, who have blue hair, and have been releasing consistently for the last 3 years, and never disappeared to go on 'normal people vacations' but have gone "all in" to keep relentless communication their fans documenting all the highs and the lows along the way"?</p><p>One.</p><p><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ashnikko" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Ashnikko.</a></p><p>What I'm trying to tell you is that you need a story.</p><p>What I'm trying to tell you is that you need to nurture your audience.</p><p>Your "people".</p><p>You have to be like that Presets song... SING IT:</p><h2>"I'm here with all of my people</h2><h2>Locked up with all of my people</h2><h2>So let me hear you scream if you're with me"</h2><p>You have to know in fine detail who you're doing this for.</p><p>Otherwise, your lyrics will suck.</p><p>Your pictures will suck.</p><p>Your art will suck.</p><p>Your brand will suck.</p><p><em>Creative work sucks when it isn't made for anyone in particular.</em></p><p>Yuck.</p><p>It sucks when... You're trying to appeal to "everybody".</p><p>Gross.</p><p>It makes you vanilla.</p><p>Instead, what you want is for your fans to say, "I feel like you made this just for me."</p><p>That's what I was talking about in this module: </p><p><a href="https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/9022531" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">LE$$XN 02: Every Brand Should Have A Muse </a></p><p>But you only read it. You understood it, but you didn't DO anything about it.</p><p>It gave you "motivation",... And then you forgot about it when your friend texted you to go to the new Ramen spot.</p><p><a href="https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/22983261" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">But reading is not action. That may have been all you needed for the other modules but you can't get away with that in 'The Book Of Money'.</a> </p><p>You need to grab a pen and paper so you can figure this shit out once and for all.</p><p>That's what a PR person would do if you had the money to hire one.</p><p><a href="https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/27097903" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">YOUR FANS ARE EVERYTHING. START ACTING LIKE IT (PART II)</a></p><p>Don't click that on that link until you have a pen and paper.</p><p>And when you do... Don't fucking just "read through it"!</p><p>This is not about "motivation" anymore.</p><p>It's about action.</p><p>The type of action that makes you feel like you've moved the needle in your career today.</p><p>This is the stuff no-one calls you out on. Good luck finding anything like what's in this 'action sheet' anywhere else on the internet.</p><p>Why? 'Cause I made this for MY people.</p><p>Got your pen and paper. Let's go:</p><p><a href="https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/27097903" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">YOUR FANS ARE EVERYTHING. START ACTING LIKE IT (PART II)</a></p><p></p>
</div>
